content:
# Per-Clip White Balance — Design Decisions (Issue #8)
## What: New pipeline script `01b_estimate_white_balance.py` that estimates per-clip white balance from keyframes using grey-world algorithm, outputs JSON consumed by Phase 5 to set Resolve Node 2 ("Neutral Balance") gains.
## Why: Static LUT applies identical WB to every clip. Underwater conditions (depth, turbidity, ambient light) vary per clip. XHS creators always do per-clip WB as first correction step.
## Key Design Choices:
1. Standalone script (not integrated into Phase 2 or 5) — follows pipeline separation-of-concerns pattern
2. Grey-world algorithm over white-patch — underwater footage lacks reliable white references
3. Pixel filtering: exclude very dark (<5% luminance) and highly saturated (>90%) pixels that skew WB estimate
4. Output to `working/white_balance/{date}/{clip_id}.json` — same pattern as scene_analysis
5. NOT depth-aware — depth maps come from Phase 4 (runs after this script), can't depend on them
## Alternatives Rejected:
- White-patch: no reliable white references in underwater footage
- Integration into Phase 2: conflates API-dependent scene analysis with pure-computation WB
- Integration into Phase 5: Phase 5 runs on host machine without numpy/PIL
- Depth-aware WB: creates circular dependency with Phase 4
